title: How to find the last value in a column
task: Get the most recent entry in a column that keeps growing — the last non-blank cell.
keywords: last non blank cell google sheets

google_sheets.note: Identical; Sheets also has =INDEX(A2:A100,COUNTA(A2:A100)) for gap-free columns.

explanation: The classic LOOKUP trick: (A2:A100<>"") is an array of TRUE/FALSE, and 1 divided by it gives 1 for filled cells and a #DIV/0! error for blanks. LOOKUP searches for 2 — larger than anything in the array — so it scans to the end and returns the value beside the last 1, i.e. the last non-blank cell. It skips gaps: in the sample, A4 is empty but the result is still "cherry" from A5.
